title: Oil in supply chain explains muted price reaction to shocks
section_path: Oil in supply chain explains muted price reaction to shocks
section_path_text: Oil in supply chain explains muted price reaction to shocks
summary:
Main
In this insight, we take a look at the global state of oil, potential challenges and projections for the markets amid rising Israel-Iran tension. By David Wech
Israel's strikes on Iran as of 12 June 2025 triggered an immediate price spike of $8/b for Brent, which receded soon to around $5/b. Given that there are concerns 1) Iran's oil infrastructure could be hit, 2) the Strait of Hormuz could be temporarily closed, and 3) there could be some type of indirect retaliation with Iranian strikes, e.g.
